context: Tests whether the agent actually starts the server and makes real HTTP requests to verify behavior — not just reads/analyzes the source code — and whether the report follows evidence rules: exact commands recorded, real responses captured, failure paths exercised, surfaces named, success kept terse, findings tied to impact, and a valid verdict produced.

checklist.name: Server started and queried
checklist.description: Report shows evidence that the server was actually started (e.g. references to starting the process) and that HTTP requests were made to localhost:9127 — not just a static code review

checklist.name: Real HTTP client used
checklist.description: Verification used curl or an equivalent real HTTP client (e.g. httpx, requests, wget) to exercise the server — not just calling Python functions directly or importing the module

checklist.name: Actual response captured
checklist.description: Report includes actual HTTP response body content and status codes (e.g. '200 OK', '404', JSON response body) received from the live server

checklist.name: Error/failure path exercised
checklist.description: At least one error or failure path was tested (e.g. requesting a non-existent user ID, sending malformed JSON to POST /users, or requesting an unknown route) and the response is documented

checklist.name: Exact surfaces named
checklist.description: Report names the specific endpoints exercised (e.g. GET /users, GET /users/1, POST /users, GET /users/999) — not just 'the API was tested'

checklist.name: Exact commands recorded
checklist.description: Report includes the actual commands run (e.g. the curl command lines with flags and URLs), not just a prose description of what was done

checklist.name: Success kept terse
checklist.description: Report does NOT include verbose output from successful requests (e.g. does not dump full response headers for every passing call); successful checks are summarized briefly

checklist.name: Finding tied to impact
checklist.description: At least one finding or observation in the report explains what could break, who is affected, or why it matters — not just a description of the behavior observed

checklist.name: Valid verdict
checklist.description: Report contains a verdict using exactly one of: 'ready for review', 'needs more work', or 'blocked'. Does NOT use 'ship it' (that verdict belongs to review).

checklist.name: Compact verification footer
checklist.description: Report keeps the final verification footer to no more than 5 labeled lines, does not repeat detailed response bodies after recording them once, and summarizes successful evidence by command or surface name
